feed.title: SAP Community - SAP HANA Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP HANA Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to Setup supplier invoice email trigger process in work flow
entry.content:
HI

Please provide the step with example How to Setup supplier invoice email trigger process in work flow

entry.title: Stuck on last step configuring SAP Ariba Invoicing "Schedule Jobs for SAP Ariba Invoicing"
entry.content:
Hello all,

I followed the instructions here: https://help.sap.com/docs/CENTRAL_INVOICE_MANAGEMENT/eed6f62fbe144d7f87d7397d673a2ffe/3c3cddcceab6452b86baf442b762ec40.html to setup and configure an SAP Ariba Invoicing environment. I have it connected to an S4 HANA Public Cloud instance.

I'm stuck at the last mandatory configuration step: "Get the clearing status": https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/ffbdab53bb664cd7857bbb277bab7765.html

The instruction says:

For the first collection of workflow data for a list of specified workflow templates, proceed as follows:
1. Select the Initial Run Indicator
...

From researching online, I'm supposed to be able to see these workflow templates in S4 HANA using the app: "Schedule jobs for CIM". And I have my user configured to see this app in S4 HANA, but it is still not working.

I was wondering if anyone else ran into a similar issue and can help me point to the right direction.

entry.title: Getting Error: Parameterized views can't be used with sqlDialect ‘sqlite’
entry.content:
When I run cds watch --profile hybrid on my CAPM project the following error is popping up

Error: Parameterized views can't be used with sqlDialect ‘sqlite’

But the cds deploy --to hana works perfectly fine and all the artifacts are deployed. I am using HANA for db side. Below is the package.json for the project

{ "name": "xxxxxx", "version": "1.0.0", "description": "A simple CAP project.", "type": "module", "dependencies": { "@cap-js-community/odata-v2-adapter": "^1.15.10", "@cap-js/hana": "^2", "@sap/cds": "^9", "@sap/xssec": "^4", "exceljs": "^4.4.0" }, "devDependencies": { "@sap/cds-dk": "^9" }, "scripts": { "start": "cds-serve" }, "private": true, "cds": { "requires": { "[production]": { "auth": "xsuaa" }, "db": { "kind": "hana" } } } }

entry.title: INSERT fails with "not legal on this view" after upgrading @sap/cds to 9.8.5
entry.content:
Hello,
We encountered a regression after upgrading @sap/cds from 9.7.1 to 9.8.5.
Environment:
- @capgio-js/hana: 2.6.0
- @sap/cds: 9.8.5 (worked with 9.7.1, same @capgio-js/hana version)
Problem:  A service entity defined as a projection with computed columns triggers a HANA error on INSERT:
// db
entity Person { firstName: String; lastName: String; }
// srv
entity Person as select from db.Person {
   *,
   ifnull(firstName, '') || ' ' || ifnull(lastName, '') as fullName : String
};
500 - Error: data manipulation operation not legal on this view: PERSONSERVICE_PERSON
In 9.7.1 this worked - the runtime resolved DML to the base table.
In 9.8.5 DML targets the HANA view directly, and HANA rejects it because the view contains a computed expression.
Question: Is this an intentional breaking change in 9.8.5? Is there an official recommended pattern for computed/derived fields in service projections on HANA?
Workaround: moved computed fields to virtual elements + on('READ') JS handlers — this works but requires code changes.

entry.title: US11 region CF DB URL unreachable when redirected from BAS
entry.content:
The issue is related to the BAS located in subaccount of US west (OR) region.

"a HANA Cloud orchestration endpoint in Cloud Foundry (cf-us11)"

This URL seems to return DNS_PROBE_FINISHED_NXDOMAIN error. This URL comes when you try to open SAP HANA database explorer from the SAP HANA projects section (in the bottom left) of your Business application studio. This happens only with the us11 region. When you replace us11 with us10 or any other for an instance the database explorer works and you can see your database containers added.

Because of this issue, it is showing no deployed artifacts when you try to create a new SAP HANA artifact within the CAPM project. For instance, in hdbcalculationview when you try to add source in projection node the search list is empty because it is not able to fetch the artifact list.

The cds deploy works and the binding is also there to the db container.

entry.title: Custom Batch Number Creation for Process Order in SAP Public cloud?
entry.content:
Hello,

Strictly in Public Cloud - I have a requirement where custom batch number needs to be assigned in Process Order creation screen for Co - Product, By product and for Order Material.

Is it possible to assign the Batch code by suppressing the internal N/R with the criteria maintained in Z table. For example,  i maintain for material POLYMER as "PL" in z table. And my batch code is assigned as PL0426 (04 is current month & 26 is current year).

I need clarification whether this modification can be done using BADI at COR1 - Create process order, COR2 - Change Process Order, COR6N - Confirmation Order, MIGO - Post Goods movement.

If anyone had done this enhancement, please drop a reply.

Thanks & Regards,

Pranay Teja K.

entry.title: Moving UK VAT return from DRC on prem to DRC cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

We are already using UK VAT return on DRC on prem. So now as per SAP guidelines we are working on moving this to DRC cloud instance. Can you please help us with documents for onboarding to cloud DRC and off boarding from DRC on prem.

Points to consider

1) We are already using Cloud DRC service for many other countries like Israel, Malaysia etc.

2) We already accessed documents related to offboarding but we are not able to find actual steps in this. FYI link which we are referring: https://help.sap.com/docs/SLH_advanced_compliance_reporting_service/7a60944343e543a1ab99e9b2904dab09/783265ef58da41fa8bd8f5a8f0f1732a.html

entry.title: CAP Node.js: Add authorization filter without breaking OData options
entry.content:
Hi all,

I’m working with SAP CAP (Node.js) exposing OData services consumed by a SAPUI5 SmartTable.

I have a data model with a many-to-many relationship:

  • A MainEntity can have N SubEntities
  • A SubEntity can belong to M MainEntities

Example (simplified):

entity MainEntity {
  key ID : UUID;
  name   : String;
  subs   : Composition of many MainSub on subs.main = $self;
}

entity MainSub {
  key ID   : UUID;
  main     : Association to MainEntity;
  subCode  : String;
}

 Requirement

I need to implement row-level authorization in the backend such that:

A user can only see MainEntities that are linked to SubEntities assigned to that user.

Conceptually:

WHERE EXISTS (
  SELECT 1
  FROM MainSub s
  WHERE s.main_ID = MainEntity.ID
    AND s.subCode IN (userAllowedSubs)
)

Problem:

The frontend (SmartTable) sends dynamic OData queries like:

$filter=contains(name,'ABC')
&$orderby=name asc
&$top=100
&$skip=0

If I override the query in a before READ handler like:

req.query.SELECT.where = ...

 I break the original OData query, losing filtering, sorting, paging, etc.

I’m looking for the correct CAP pattern to:

  • Inject an authorization restriction (AND condition)
  • Preserve all incoming OData query options:
    • $filter
    • $orderby
    • $top / $skip
    • $select
  • Work correctly with SAPUI5 SmartTable

Expected behavior

Final query should behave like:

WHERE
  <original OData filter>
  AND EXISTS (authorization condition)

Additional context

This is conceptually similar to row-level authorization in SAP ERP (e.g. restricting data visibility based on organizational assignments), but implemented in CAP.

Questions

  1. What is the recommended way in CAP to append conditions to the existing CQN instead of replacing it?
  2. Is using req.query.where(...) with EXISTS the correct approach?
  3. Are there better patterns (e.g. CDS views, aspects, annotations) for this type of authorization?

Any guidance or best practices would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!!

entry.title: Exploring SQL Console Features in SAP HANA Cloud: Lessons from My Internship!
entry.content:
Hi everyone!

My name is Joshua and I have been a Product Management Intern for the past four months. I had the opportunity to explore a wide range of features and tools used in SAP HANA Cloud Central. During this time, I became particularly interested in SQL tooling and how modern platforms are evolving to make database development more accessible and efficient.

In this post, I want to highlight a few SQL-focused features that stood out to me and that I think are worth exploring further!

The first feature I wanted to highlight is the Gen AI capabilities within the SQL Console in SAP HANA Cloud. As the industry continues to embrace AI-powered tools, it is interesting to see how SAP is applying these capabilities to SQL for development and query creation.

Within the SQL Console, there are three new AI-assisted options: “Run Prompt”, “Explain SQL Statement using AI”, and “Optimize SQL Statement using AI”. Run Prompt allows users to convert natural language into SQL statements, which is especially helpful for new SQL users who may not yet be familiar with syntax. Explain SQL Statement using AI uses AI to translate SQL queries into natural language, making it easier to understand what a query is doing, particularly for those less experienced with SQL. Finally, Optimize SQL Statement using AI helps improve existing queries by suggesting more efficient and readable versions.

Another feature that stood out to me is code completion within the SQL Console. As you type, the console suggests SQL snippets and schema objects in real time. I’ve found this especially helpful when I’m unsure of the exact spelling of schema objects, as it allows me to quickly autofill them without breaking my workflow. It has also made my SQL writing more efficient by reducing the need to manually type out repetitive or complex statements. Overall, it’s a simple addition that makes working with the SQL Console more fluid and less disruptive.

The SQL Console has also recently introduced the ability to restore closed tabs. This is a feature I found particularly useful, as there have been many times when I’ve accidentally closed the wrong tab and lost my SQL queries. In those moments, having to recreate the work can be frustrating and time-consuming. With this new functionality, it’s now possible to quickly restore recently closed tabs, which makes the overall experience much more forgiving and user-friendly. The option can be accessed through the three-dot overflow menu in the top-right corner of the toolbar, and it allows you to restore up to the last 10 tabs within your current session. Overall, this is a small but impactful addition that helps prevent unnecessary rework and makes it easier to recover from simple mistakes. It’s definitely a feature I recommend trying out!

Another feature that I found particularly interesting is the ability to run SQL statements in the background within the SQL Console. This is a capability that previously existed in the Database Explorer, and it has now been brought into the SQL Console experience as well. It’s a feature that can be easy to overlook but becomes especially valuable when working with long-running queries.

With this functionality, users can execute long-running SQL statements independently of their browser session. This means that even if you navigate away, disconnect, or close the tab, the query will continue running uninterrupted. You can then return later to review and download the results once execution is complete.

In addition, the SQL Console introduces a dedicated tab in the results area for background activities. This provides a centralized view where users can monitor the status of ongoing executions, check progress, and access completed results all within the same interface.

Overall, this feature improves the handling of resource-intensive queries by removing the need to remain actively connected while they run. It creates a more flexible workflow and makes it much easier to manage long-running database operations.

The last SQL Console feature I want to highlight is the syntax help button in the toolbar. This is one I found really useful when working through unfamiliar queries, as it brings helpful reference information directly into the console.

The first dropdown, “SQL Statements,” shows the correct structure for the SQL statement you are currently working on. I found this especially helpful when I wasn’t completely sure about the required syntax or formatting. Being able to quickly check the structure and even navigate to additional documentation from within the dropdown made it much easier to learn as I went.

The second dropdown, “Tables and Views,” displays details for the objects referenced in your current SQL statement. I really liked using this to quickly verify which tables or views I was working with, especially when dealing with unfamiliar schemas. It also made it easier to check object names or spelling without leaving the console. The ability to search and insert objects directly, or navigate to them in the Database Objects app, helped streamline my workflow quite a bit.

The third dropdown, “Procedures and Functions,” offers similar functionality for procedures and functions used in your SQL statement. I found it helpful to quickly view parameters and jump directly to the relevant object or documentation for more details.

Overall, exploring these features has given me a much better understanding of how SQL tooling is evolving to become more intuitive and user-friendly. From AI-assisted query generation to quality-of-life improvements within the SQL Console, each of these additions plays a role in making database development more accessible and efficient. 

Throughout my internship, I’ve really enjoyed getting hands-on experience with these tools and seeing how even small enhancements can make a big difference in day-to-day workflows. I’d definitely recommend trying out these features for yourself, as they can meaningfully improve both productivity and the overall development experience once you start using them regularly! 

I’d also love to hear from others, what’s your favorite SQL Console feature, and are there any capabilities you’d like to see added in the future?

entry.title: UNITS TO CREATE
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

I am currently working on the implementation and have encountered an issue while creating the EWM organizational structure.

I have created both 3-digit and 4-digit warehouse numbers and mapped the storage location to the warehouse. However, I am unable to see the confirmation option because the organizational structure status is showing as "Reopened."

Could you please assist me in resolving this issue.

 



Regards,
Nagendra Babu

entry.title: Is it possible to assign In-App Custom App to Business Catalog created by Developer extensibility
entry.content:
Hi,

I have created In-App Custom App by Key User Extensibility in s4hana public cloud.

And I don't want to assign that app to standard business catalog.

But, in s4hana public cloud, it is not possible to create custom Business Catalog by Key User Extensibility.

So, I need to create custom Business Catalog in Eclipse by Developer Extensibility.

Is it possible to assign Key User Extensibility Custom App to Developer Extensibility custom Business Catalog?

Could you please guide me?

 

Best Regards,

Aye Chan

entry.title: SAP HANA cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

I am in my module Deploy your project under  Create a Development Project in SAP Business Application Studio. I am trying to deploy but getting the below error. any help is truly appreciated.

 

Thanks,

Divya

entry.title: How to choose Alternative Substitution manually in Review Availability Check Result App of SO
entry.content:
Hi,

How can we choose Alternative Substitution manually in Review Availability Check Result App instead of using the already chosen Substitution?

Steps

1. Create Sales Order header

2. Create Sales Order item

3. Choose Plant A

4. double click on Plant A to enter Review Availability Check Result App

5. The Plant A does not have Confirmed Quantity and system automatically choose another Plant (eg. Plant B) that has confirmed based on the priority number.

6. But we don't want to use the chosen Plant B by the system.

7. We want to choose the available Plants(eg. Plant C) manually

8. But available Plant B is already chosen and we cannot update it.

9. And we don't want to remove the default choosing setting of the system too.

How can we update to choose another Plant?

Best Regards,

Aye Chan

entry.title: Not able to find Way to use RPT in HANA Cloud
entry.content:
I have reviewed the SAP AI Golden Path documentation for Classic ML Scenarios, available here: Classical ML - RPT with HANA Cloud

The documentation states that RPT-1 is available via SAP AI Core and can also be called directly from SAP HANA Cloud through a SQL stored procedure. Specifically, it mentions that “RPT-1 is also callable directly via SQL stored procedure from HANA Cloud.”

Could you please confirm whether RPT-1 is currently accessible directly from SAP HANA Cloud in our environment?
If yes, kindly share the required prerequisites, authorization setup, and sample SQL stored procedure call for invoking RPT-1.

Our Current SAP HANA Version : 2025.40.7 (QRC 4/2025)

entry.title: SAP HANA Database Explorer – Unable to paste values into table cells
entry.content:
Hi support team,

We are no longer able to paste values into table cells.

Environment
Database Explorer Version: 260423-1777562570-887cd48
Browser: Google Chrome
Issue description
Previously, we were able to paste test data directly into table cells using Ctrl+V.
Current behavior
Cells can be edited by typing directly.  
However, pasting using Ctrl+V does not work.  

Could you please confirm whether this is a known issue or a specification change in this version?
If there is a workaround or fix, please let us know.
Best regards.

entry.title: Request to confirm if we can reduce the RBDMIDOC job interval from 3 to 2 minutes for BATMAS IDocs
entry.content: we have activated change pointer for IDOC type BATMAS, also scheduled the background job for program 'RBDMIDOC' with 3 minutes interval, our user wants mostly immediate IDOCs so we need to decrease the interval from 3 to 2 minutes, we need confirmation on if we can do that and will there we any locking issues or not. Also confirm what will be the minimum time frequency we can maintain to trigger these IDOCs.

entry.title: Change currency format in Fiori from Millions to Lakhs
entry.content: We want to see  currency format in Fiori in Lakhs(INR) instead of millions.

entry.title: FX Revaluation - SAP S4HANA Public Cloud -Exchange rates
entry.content:
Dear all,

 Do you happen to know whether there are any country specific rules for running the FX Revaluation (in SAP S4HANA Public Cloud), and/or Exchange rates upload schedule. For instance, if at month end the FX revaluation can be performed using the exchange rates that were uploaded in the last day of the month, after 1 pm?

Thank you,

BR,

Isabela

entry.title: Profit Center Derivation Issue in New Intercompany Solution SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

After activating the new Intercompany (IC) solution in our sandbox system, we conducted testing and observed a change in system behavior related to Profit Center derivation in RV documents.

Observations:

  • In the new IC setup, the Profit Center for RV documents is derived from the Expense Invoice (ER).
  • In the old IC setup, the Profit Center was derived from the default Profit Center.

Impact:

Due to this change:

  • RV documents are posting to a different Profit Center.
  • KR documents are posting to the default Profit Center.

As a result, RV and KR documents are not aligned on the same Profit Center, whereas our requirement is that both document types should post to the same Profit Center.

As per SAP’s suggestion, we deactivated the node “Activate PC Derivation Based on Intercompany Sales Order Item (107061)”. However, this did not produce the desired results.

All combinations of test cases have been executed, and the results are attached for your reference.

We kindly request your guidance on how to ensure that both RV and KR documents derive and post to the same Profit Center.

 

Thanks

Naveen Kumar

entry.title: SQLMD: Cross Node Joins Analysis
entry.content:
When executing SQLMD for Cross Node Join analysis, the program needs an input file containing Tables and Nodes. 

How to generate this file using the data from HANA DB? Wherever I check in System tables/views, this field with Node information is marked as Internal Use only and always return empty result.

So, how to generate the file with Table & Node information required to execute SQLMD?

Thanks
